# TestSpec Agent Workflow

<!-- Generated by TestSpec. Keep this marker to allow `testspec init` to refresh this file. -->

Configured integrations: Claude Code, Codex.

When the user asks for a TestSpec workflow label, run the backing CLI command:

| Workflow label  | Backing CLI command                        | Purpose                                                           |
| --------------- | ------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `test:new`      | `testspec new <name> --requirement <path>` | Create a test proposal workspace from a requirement document.     |
| `test:analysis` | `testspec analysis [name]`                 | Decompose requirements into testable items, risks, and questions. |
| `test:points`   | `testspec points [name]`                   | Generate core scenario test points for a test change.             |
| `test:excel`    | `testspec excel [name]`                    | Export executable Excel test cases.                               |
| `test:mind`     | `testspec mind [name]`                     | Export mind-map style test cases for review.                      |
| `test:report`   | `testspec report [name]`                   | Generate execution statistics from Excel results.                 |
| `test:archive`  | `testspec archive [name]`                  | Archive the full test artifact chain for traceability.            |

Recommended order:

1. `test:new` creates `testspec/changes/<name>/proposal.md`.
2. `test:analysis` creates `requirements-analysis.md`.
3. `test:points` creates `specs/testpoints.md`.
4. `test:excel` creates `artifacts/<name>_cases.xlsx`.
5. `test:mind` creates `artifacts/<name>_cases.xmind`.
6. `test:report` creates `report.md` after Excel execution results are filled.
7. `test:archive` moves the change to `testspec/changes/archive/<date>-<name>/`.

Important: `test:*` entries are Agent workflow labels, not shell commands. Use `testspec <subcommand>` in the terminal.

<!-- END TESTSPEC AGENT WORKFLOW -->
